user_content: I'm planning to plant my seedlings in the raised bed I recently built. Do you have any tips on how to harden off my seedlings before transferring them outdoors? By the way, the raised bed was made using a specific type of wood that was previously used for another purpose, so I'm curious if that might affect the soil quality or plant growth.

I've been enjoying the harvest immensely! I planted 5 tomato plants initially, and they've been producing like crazy. I've been harvesting tomatoes almost daily, and they're so delicious and flavorful. I've been using them in salads, sandwiches, and cooking up a storm in the kitchen. It's amazing how much more flavorful homegrown tomatoes are compared to store-bought ones.

I'm planning to use the compost to fertilize my tomato plants. Do you have any specific tips on how to fertilize tomato plants, and are there any signs I should look out for to determine if my tomato plants are getting enough nutrients?

I've been doing my best to keep my tomato plants healthy, but I did notice that some of the leaves started to turn yellow and wilt recently. I did some research and realized I might be overwatering them. So, I cut back on the watering schedule, and it seems to be working - the plants are looking healthier again.
